verification.home_manager_build: pass (7 derivations built incl. mail-sync.drv; result symlink produced)
verification.concurrency_test: pass (run twice, non-overlapping [START,END] windows both times, exit 0, lockfile present, no reentrancy)

completion_summary: Created a single canonical, flock-serialized, group-scoped mail-sync wrapper (modules/home/email/mail-sync.nix) structurally incapable of mbsync -a, wired it into modules/home/default.nix, repointed notmuch.nix's preNew hook and aerc.nix's $ keybind to call it, and verified via nix flake check, home-manager build --flake .#benjamin, and a credential-free stub-mbsync concurrency test proving serialization.
next_steps: Run 'home-manager switch --flake .#benjamin' to activate, then exercise mail-sync gmail/logos/both manually against the real account before relying on it in production triggers.
